decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip hot spot.

changes.reason: Doubling the spreader conductivity (within the allowed 2.0x step) significantly improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation.
